key_concepts.salvage: The process of identifying valuable parts of a failed or under-implemented system and rebuilding around them.
key_concepts.mvp_recovery: Choosing the smallest valuable use case that leverages existing working components and can ship quickly.
key_concepts.stub_codebase: A project with clean structure and many components but no real implementation.
key_concepts.foundation_first: Building production infrastructure (auth, storage, APIs, observability) before adding AI features.
key_concepts.buy_vs_build: Using existing frameworks (LangChain, FastAPI, Prometheus) instead of custom implementations.
key_concepts.least_privilege_connectors: Connectors that default to read-only access and require explicit approval for writes.

keep: Working infrastructure that does what it claims (loaders, registries, validators, real graph engines)
Accurate reference data (breaking changes, best practices, checklists)
merge: Multiple engines that apply the same pattern (simulation, incident, optimization)
Reasoning, planning, and decision into one intelligence layer
Execution, workflow, and tool adapters into one runtime
delete: Engines that only return hardcoded sample data
Fake connectors with no real integration
Duplicate data structures for the same concept

recommended_stack.ai: OpenAI/Anthropic via LiteLLM + LangChain/LangGraph + sentence-transformers + pgvector

connector_security.postgresql: Read-only default, SSL, query timeout, least-privilege user, no DDL unless approved
connector_security.odoo_orm: XML-RPC/JSON-RPC with authenticated user, impersonation logging, no direct db writes
connector_security.git: SSH keys per tenant, branch protection checks, read-only default
connector_security.docker: Docker socket restricted, no privileged containers, audit all execs
connector_security.kubernetes: Service account per tenant, RBAC, namespace isolation, no cluster-admin

phases.name: Foundation
phases.focus: PostgreSQL, Redis, FastAPI, auth, multi-tenancy, audit logs, real read-only connectors

phases.name: Real Digital Twin
phases.focus: Graph DB backend, event-driven sync, delta history, staleness detection

phases.name: AI Layer
phases.focus: LLM integration, RAG, embeddings, vector memory, human-in-the-loop

phases.name: Execution
phases.focus: Sandboxed workflow execution, staging promotion, rollback, migration dry-run

phases.name: Operations
phases.focus: Real monitoring, incident response, optimization from actual data, enterprise dashboard

phases.name: Scale
phases.focus: Horizontal scaling, fleet management, enterprise governance
